meaningEn: to remember or consider something
examples: Keep in mind that the deadline is Friday.
Please keep in mind that users may have slow internet.
Keep in mind that clear communication saves time.

conversation.question: Should we release it today?
conversation.answer: Yes, but keep in mind that QA has not tested it yet.

meaningEn: to think of an idea or plan
examples: Can you come up with a better title?
The team came up with a simple solution.
I need to come up with examples for my presentation.

conversation.question: Do we have any ideas for the homepage?
conversation.answer: Not yet, but I'll come up with a few options today.

meaningEn: to be clear, logical, or easy to understand
examples: Your explanation makes sense.
This folder structure makes sense for a small app.
It makes sense to practice speaking every day.

conversation.question: Should we split this component?
conversation.answer: Yes, it makes sense because it is getting too large.

meaningEn: to handle or manage a problem or situation
examples: I need to deal with some emails first.
The app should deal with empty data safely.
Good leaders know how to deal with pressure.

conversation.question: Can you fix the login issue?
conversation.answer: Yes, I'll deal with it after the standup.

meaningEn: to mention or show something important
examples: She pointed out a mistake in my report.
The reviewer pointed out a missing test case.
I want to point out one risk before we continue.

conversation.question: Did anyone notice the bug?
conversation.answer: Yes, Huy pointed it out during review.

meaningEn: a small problem
examples: It is just a minor issue.
We found a minor issue in the UI.
A minor issue can become serious if ignored.

conversation.question: Is this bug serious?
conversation.answer: No, it's a minor issue. I can fix it quickly.

meaningEn: to become familiar with something
examples: You'll get used to the new schedule.
It takes time to get used to a large codebase.
I'm getting used to speaking English in meetings.

conversation.question: Is Cursor hard to use?
conversation.answer: A little at first, but you'll get used to it.

meaningEn: to mention a topic
examples: She brought up an interesting point.
I will bring up the API issue in the meeting.
Don't be afraid to bring up your ideas.

conversation.question: Should I mention the delay?
conversation.answer: Yes, bring it up clearly in the meeting.

meaningEn: to divide something into smaller parts or explain simply
meaningVi: chia nhỏ, giải thích từng phần
examples: Let's break down the problem.
The mentor broke down the architecture step by step.
I break down new vocabulary into meaning, form, and examples.

conversation.question: This task feels too big.
conversation.answer: Let's break it down into smaller tickets.

meaningEn: to contact someone again or continue after something
examples: I'll follow up with you tomorrow.
Please follow up with QA after the fix.
A good learner follows up on mistakes.

conversation.question: Did Mike reply?
conversation.answer: Not yet. I'll follow up later today.

meaningEn: to investigate or examine something
examples: I'll look into the problem.
Can you look into the slow page load?
You should look into better ways to practice speaking.

conversation.question: Why is the build failing?
conversation.answer: I'm not sure. I'll look into it now.

meaningEn: to experience a problem unexpectedly
examples: We ran into a problem during testing.
I ran into a TypeScript error.
Learners often run into pronunciation problems.

conversation.question: Did the deployment succeed?
conversation.answer: Not yet. We ran into a build issue.

meaningEn: to find or meet something by chance
examples: I came across a useful article.
I came across this bug while testing.
You may come across many new expressions in meetings.

conversation.question: Where did you find this phrase?
conversation.answer: I came across it in a podcast.

meaningEn: to review, examine, or experience something
examples: Let's go through the document together.
I went through the PR comments this morning.
When learning words, go through examples carefully.

conversation.question: Can you explain the ticket?
conversation.answer: Sure, let's go through it step by step.

meaningEn: to create or organize something by combining parts
examples: I put together a list of ideas.
She put together a quick demo.
I put together examples to practice speaking.

conversation.question: Do we have a report?
conversation.answer: Not yet, but I'll put one together.

meaningEn: to record or remember information
examples: I use an app to keep track of expenses.
We need to keep track of user progress.
Keep track of new words you learn every day.

conversation.question: How do you remember vocab?
conversation.answer: I keep track of it in my review app.

meaningEn: to know that something exists or matters
examples: Be aware of your tone when speaking.
Developers should be aware of accessibility issues.
Be aware of common pronunciation mistakes.

conversation.question: Should we worry about mobile users?
conversation.answer: Yes, we should be aware of their experience.

meaningEn: because of something that happened
examples: He practiced daily; as a result, he improved.
The API was slow; as a result, the page loaded late.
I reviewed vocabulary every night; as a result, I remembered more words.

conversation.question: Did the new cache help?
conversation.answer: Yes, as a result, the page feels much faster.

meaningEn: used to show a different or opposite idea
examples: The tool is powerful. On the other hand, it can be expensive.
Remote work is flexible. On the other hand, communication is harder.
AI helps learning. On the other hand, you still need practice.

conversation.question: Should we use a complex solution?
conversation.answer: It is scalable. On the other hand, it may slow us down.

meaningEn: used to say the answer changes based on conditions
examples: It depends on your schedule.
It depends on the project requirements.
It depends on how often you review the words.

conversation.question: Should we use Zustand?
conversation.answer: It depends on the complexity of the state.

meaningEn: to be expected or required to do something
examples: You are supposed to submit it today.
The button is supposed to open a modal.
This app is supposed to help you review vocabulary.

conversation.question: Why is this hidden?
conversation.answer: It is supposed to be hidden until the user clicks Show.

meaningEn: used to talk about a specific aspect
examples: In terms of cost, this option is better.
In terms of performance, this component needs improvement.
In terms of speaking, examples matter a lot.

conversation.question: Which tool is better?
conversation.answer: In terms of coding speed, Cursor is stronger.

meaningEn: because of
examples: The flight was delayed due to bad weather.
The page is slow due to large images.
I missed practice due to a busy schedule.

conversation.question: Why did the test fail?
conversation.answer: It failed due to a missing mock.

meaningEn: used to add another idea
examples: The app is useful. In addition, it is easy to use.
We fixed the bug. In addition, we added tests.
In addition, you should practice speaking with examples.

conversation.question: Did you update the UI?
conversation.answer: Yes. In addition, I improved the empty state.

meaningEn: for example
examples: Some habits are useful; for instance, daily review.
Frontend tools change quickly; for instance, React has new patterns.
Use practical phrases; for instance, 'from my perspective'.

conversation.question: Can you give an example?
conversation.answer: For instance, you can use this phrase in meetings.

meaningEn: correct and exact
examples: The answer is accurate.
We need accurate user data.
Accurate pronunciation helps speaking sound clearer.

conversation.question: Is this translation correct?
conversation.answer: Yes, it looks accurate.

meaningEn: able to change easily according to needs
examples: My schedule is flexible.
The layout should be flexible on mobile.
A flexible learning plan is easier to maintain.

conversation.question: Can we change the meeting time?
conversation.answer: Yes, I'm flexible this afternoon.

meaningEn: useful, important, or worth a lot
examples: Your feedback is valuable.
Code review is valuable for learning.
Speaking practice is valuable if you want real fluency.

conversation.question: Was the meeting useful?
conversation.answer: Yes, we got valuable feedback.

meaningEn: difficult but interesting
examples: The task is challenging.
Debugging production issues can be challenging.
Learning collocations is challenging but useful.

conversation.question: Is the project easy?
conversation.answer: Not really, but it is challenging in a good way.

meaningEn: useful in real situations
examples: This is practical advice.
We need a practical solution.
Use practical examples when learning vocabulary.

conversation.question: Should I memorize random words?
conversation.answer: No, focus on practical expressions first.

meaningEn: in a correct or suitable way
examples: Please install it properly.
The form should validate properly.
You need to review words properly, not just read them once.

conversation.question: Is the app working?
conversation.answer: Not properly. The filter is broken.

meaningEn: in the end, after some time
examples: Eventually, you will improve.
The bug was eventually fixed.
If you practice daily, you will eventually speak more naturally.

conversation.question: Did you solve it?
conversation.answer: Yes, eventually I found the root cause.

meaningEn: slowly over a period of time
examples: Your English will gradually improve.
We gradually migrated the old components.
Vocabulary grows gradually through review.

conversation.question: How can I reach B2?
conversation.answer: Gradually build your vocabulary and speaking confidence.

meaningEn: using few words or for a short time
examples: Can you explain it briefly?
Briefly, the issue is caused by state mismatch.
Briefly review new words every morning.

conversation.question: Can you summarize the ticket?
conversation.answer: Sure, I'll explain it briefly.

meaningEn: at the present time
examples: I'm currently working on a side project.
The feature is currently behind a flag.
I'm currently reviewing B1 vocabulary.

conversation.question: Are you available?
conversation.answer: Not now. I'm currently in a meeting.

meaningEn: more than usual or more than others
examples: I enjoy reading, especially about technology.
This issue matters especially on mobile.
Speaking examples are especially useful for learners.

conversation.question: Is this important?
conversation.answer: Yes, especially for new users.

meaningEn: to need work, time, or energy
examples: Learning English requires effort.
Building a clean app requires effort.
Improving speaking requires effort and patience.

conversation.question: Is this skill easy to learn?
conversation.answer: Not exactly. It requires effort.

meaningEn: to improve or move forward
examples: You are making progress.
The team made progress on the feature.
I make progress when I review vocabulary daily.

conversation.question: Am I improving?
conversation.answer: Yes, you are making progress every week.

meaningEn: to become more confident over time
examples: Practice helps build confidence.
Small wins build confidence in coding.
Speaking with examples can build confidence.

conversation.question: I'm nervous in meetings.
conversation.answer: Practice short answers to build confidence.

meaningEn: to communicate thoughts clearly
examples: She can express ideas clearly.
Good vocabulary helps you express ideas at work.
I want to express ideas naturally in English.

conversation.question: Why learn collocations?
conversation.answer: They help you express ideas more naturally.

meaningEn: to achieve something you want
examples: You can reach your goal with practice.
We reached our sprint goal.
To reach a language goal, you need a clear plan.

conversation.question: Can I get to B2 this year?
conversation.answer: Yes, if you practice consistently, you can reach that goal.

meaningEn: a mistake many people make
examples: This is a common mistake.
A common mistake is forgetting loading states.
Using only single words is a common mistake in vocabulary learning.

conversation.question: Why does this sound unnatural?
conversation.answer: It's a common mistake with collocations.

meaningEn: an easy-to-understand explanation
examples: Thanks for the clear explanation.
The PR needs a clear explanation.
A clear explanation helps learners remember new words.

conversation.question: Did my answer help?
conversation.answer: Yes, it was a clear explanation.

meaningEn: easy to read and understand
examples: The text is readable.
Readable code is easier to review.
Readable examples help learners remember words.

conversation.question: Is my code okay?
conversation.answer: Yes, but make it more readable.

meaningEn: to make progress or continue
examples: Let's move forward with the plan.
We can move forward after QA approves it.
To improve English, keep moving forward even slowly.

conversation.question: Should we wait?
conversation.answer: No, let's move forward with the MVP.

meaningEn: to make less progress than expected
examples: Don't fall behind in class.
The project fell behind schedule.
If you stop reviewing, you may fall behind.

conversation.question: Are we on track?
conversation.answer: Not really. We're starting to fall behind.

meaningEn: to reach the same level after being behind
examples: I need to catch up on lessons.
We need to catch up with the sprint plan.
I missed practice yesterday, so I need to catch up today.

conversation.question: Did you finish the tasks?
conversation.answer: Not yet, but I'll catch up tonight.

meaningEn: to understand or solve something after thinking
examples: I cannot figure out this error message.
We need to figure out why users leave the page.
She figured out a faster way to review vocabulary.

conversation.question: Do you know what caused the bug?
conversation.answer: Not yet, but I will figure it out after checking the logs.

meaningEn: to discover information or learn a fact
examples: I want to find out the exam date.
We found out the issue was a typo.
Let us find out what the client needs first.

conversation.question: When is the meeting room free?
conversation.answer: I will find out and tell you in a minute.

meaningEn: to stop trying to do something
examples: Do not give up after one bad test.
He gave up smoking last year.
I almost gave up, but my teacher encouraged me.

conversation.question: English feels too hard lately.
conversation.answer: Do not give up. Small daily practice really helps.

meaningEn: to learn something informally or collect someone or something
examples: Children pick up languages quickly.
Can you pick me up at the station?
I picked up a few useful phrases at work.

conversation.question: How did you learn those idioms?
conversation.answer: I picked them up from podcasts during my commute.

meaningEn: to happen in a particular way, often unexpectedly
examples: The presentation turned out well.
It turned out that the file was missing.
The team event turned out better than we expected.

conversation.question: Was the demo stressful?
conversation.answer: A little, but it turned out fine in the end.

meaningEn: to solve a problem or have a successful result
examples: I hope everything works out for you.
We can work out a better schedule together.
The plan did not work out as expected.

conversation.question: Can we finish by Friday?
conversation.answer: I think we can work out a plan if we start today.

meaningEn: to continue doing something
examples: Please carry on with your work.
She carried on studying after dinner.
Let us carry on and fix the next bug.

conversation.question: Should we stop for lunch now?
conversation.answer: Let us carry on for ten more minutes, then eat.

meaningEn: to organize something or solve a problem
examples: I need to sort out my notes.
IT will sort out the login issue.
We sorted out the misunderstanding quickly.

conversation.question: Why is access still blocked?
conversation.answer: Support is sorting it out now.

meaningEn: to complete a form or replace someone temporarily
examples: Please fill in this form online.
Can you fill in for me tomorrow?
I filled in the missing details later.

conversation.question: Did you submit the application?
conversation.answer: Yes, I filled in the form this morning.

meaningEn: to record something on paper or digitally
examples: Write down new words in a notebook.
She wrote down the main ideas from the talk.
I always write down corrections from my teacher.

conversation.question: How do you remember new phrases?
conversation.answer: I write them down and review them every evening.

meaningEn: to go or do something at a lower speed
examples: Please slow down when you explain.
Traffic slowed down after the rain.
I need to slow down and check my answers.

conversation.question: You are speaking too fast.
conversation.answer: Sorry, I will slow down.

meaningEn: to go or happen faster
examples: We need to speed up the release process.
The lesson sped up in the second half.
Practice can speed up your progress.

conversation.question: We are behind schedule.
conversation.answer: Let us speed up testing this afternoon.

meaningEn: to use all of something so none is left
examples: We ran out of time in the meeting.
I ran out of coffee this morning.
The app ran out of storage space.

conversation.question: Can we add one more feature?
conversation.answer: Not today. We have run out of time.

meaningEn: to have a friendly relationship with someone
examples: She gets along with her teammates.
Do you get along with your manager?
It is easier to work when people get along.

conversation.question: How is your new colleague?
conversation.answer: Great. We get along with each other really well.

meaningEn: to take care of someone or something
examples: Can you look after my bag?
She looks after her younger brother.
Good developers look after code quality.

conversation.question: Who will handle support tonight?
conversation.answer: Mai will look after it until midnight.

meaningEn: to remove or throw away something unwanted
examples: We should get rid of unused files.
I want to get rid of this bad habit.
They got rid of the old design system.

conversation.question: Why is the page so slow?
conversation.answer: We need to get rid of some heavy scripts.

meaningEn: to give completed work to a teacher or manager
examples: Please hand in your essay by noon.
I handed in the report yesterday.
Students must hand in homework on time.

conversation.question: Is the assignment late?
conversation.answer: No, I handed it in this morning.

meaningEn: to search for information in a book or online
examples: Look up the word in a dictionary.
I looked up the address online.
When in doubt, look it up before guessing.

conversation.question: What does this phrase mean?
conversation.answer: I am not sure. Let us look it up together.

meaningEn: to reserve time or money for a purpose
examples: Set aside thirty minutes for review.
She sets aside money every month.
I set aside Sunday for family time.

conversation.question: When will you practice speaking?
conversation.answer: I set aside time every evening after dinner.

meaningEn: to leave the ground or become successful quickly
examples: The plane takes off at six.
Her channel took off last year.
Sales took off after the update.

conversation.question: Is the new feature popular?
conversation.answer: Yes, usage took off within a week.

meaningEn: to listen and watch carefully
examples: Pay attention to the instructions.
Students should pay attention in class.
Pay attention to how native speakers link words.

conversation.question: I keep missing details in listening.
conversation.answer: Try to pay attention to stressed words.

meaningEn: to handle a task or look after someone
examples: I will take care of the booking.
She takes care of her parents on weekends.
DevOps takes care of server updates.

conversation.question: Who will update production?
conversation.answer: The platform team will take care of it.

meaningEn: to check that something is true or done
examples: Make sure the door is locked.
Make sure you save your work often.
I made sure everyone understood the task.

conversation.question: Did you send the invite?
conversation.answer: Yes, and I made sure the time zone was correct.

meaningEn: to happen at a particular time or location
examples: The workshop takes place on Tuesday.
The change took place overnight.
Most meetings take place online now.

conversation.question: When is the interview?
conversation.answer: It takes place at ten tomorrow morning.

meaningEn: responsible for managing something
examples: She is in charge of onboarding.
Who is in charge of this project?
I am in charge of weekly reports.

conversation.question: Who approves the budget?
conversation.answer: Finance is in charge of that process.

meaningEn: to try hard to do something
examples: Make an effort to speak every day.
He made an effort to arrive early.
Small efforts add up over time.

conversation.question: My pronunciation is still weak.
conversation.answer: Make an effort to record yourself once a week.

meaningEn: to use an opportunity in a helpful way
examples: Take advantage of free library resources.
We took advantage of the holiday to rest.
Take advantage of quiet mornings to study.

conversation.question: Any tips for learning faster?
conversation.answer: Take advantage of short practice sessions.

meaningEn: to contact someone by phone, email, or message
examples: I will get in touch after the meeting.
Get in touch if you need help.
She got in touch with the supplier today.

conversation.question: How can I reach support?
conversation.answer: Get in touch through the contact form.

meaningEn: to continue communicating with someone
examples: Let us keep in touch after the course.
We keep in touch on social media.
I try to keep in touch with old classmates.

conversation.question: Are you leaving the team?
conversation.answer: Yes, but let us keep in touch.

meaningEn: at the planned or expected time
examples: The train arrived on time.
Please submit reports on time.
Being on time shows respect.

conversation.question: Were you late for the call?
conversation.answer: No, I joined on time.

meaningEn: to give most attention to one thing
examples: Focus on one skill at a time.
The lesson focuses on listening.
Let us focus on the main problem first.

conversation.question: There are too many tasks today.
conversation.answer: Focus on the release blocker first.

meaningEn: to be affected or decided by something else
examples: Results depend on practice time.
The deadline depends on approval.
Success depends on consistent effort.

conversation.question: Can we launch this week?
conversation.answer: It depends on final testing.

meaningEn: having the duty to deal with something
examples: He is responsible for client calls.
Who is responsible for this bug?
You are responsible for your own progress.

conversation.question: Who fixes production issues?
conversation.answer: The on-call engineer is responsible for them.

meaningEn: knowing about something fairly well
examples: Are you familiar with this tool?
I am familiar with basic grammar rules.
She is familiar with customer needs.

conversation.question: Can you use Git?
conversation.answer: Yes, I am familiar with the basics.

meaningEn: wanting to know or learn about something
examples: I am interested in design systems.
Are you interested in joining the club?
Many learners are interested in business English.

conversation.question: Why did you choose this course?
conversation.answer: I am interested in improving workplace communication.

meaningEn: to cause a particular situation
examples: Poor planning can result in delays.
The bug resulted in lost data.
Practice can result in better fluency.

conversation.question: Why did traffic spike fail?
conversation.answer: A config error resulted in timeouts.

meaningEn: to cause something to happen
examples: Stress can lead to mistakes.
Good habits lead to steady progress.
This shortcut may lead to more bugs.

conversation.question: Will skipping tests help us ship faster?
conversation.answer: It might, but it can lead to bigger problems later.

meaningEn: to make a formal request for a job or place
examples: She applied for an internship.
I want to apply for a scholarship.
He applied for three roles last month.

conversation.question: Any news about the job?
conversation.answer: I applied for it yesterday and am waiting to hear back.

meaningEn: to get ready for an event or task
examples: Prepare for the interview tonight.
We prepared for the product launch.
I prepare for class by reviewing notes.

conversation.question: Are you ready for the presentation?
conversation.answer: Yes, I prepared for it all week.

meaningEn: done intentionally, not by accident
examples: He did not ignore you on purpose.
I left early on purpose to avoid traffic.
Was that change made on purpose?

conversation.question: Why did you delete the file?
conversation.answer: I did it on purpose because it was outdated.

meaningEn: without intending to do something
examples: I sent the email by accident.
She found the solution by accident.
He deleted the row by accident.

conversation.question: Did you mean to post that message?
conversation.answer: No, I shared it by accident.

meaningEn: as a minimum amount or in the worst case
examples: Read at least one article daily.
We need at least two reviewers.
At least the app still works.

conversation.question: How long should I study?
conversation.answer: At least twenty minutes a day.

meaningEn: despite the fact that
examples: Even though it rained, we went out.
She passed even though she was nervous.
I stayed even though I was tired.

conversation.question: Why continue the meeting?
conversation.answer: Even though time was short, we needed a decision.

meaningEn: in addition to
examples: She speaks French as well as English.
We test features as well as performance.

conversation.question: What skills does the role need?
conversation.answer: Coding as well as communication skills.

meaningEn: for example
examples: Bring tools such as a notebook and pen.
Avoid distractions such as social media.
Topics such as travel are easy to discuss.

conversation.question: What topics can we practice?
conversation.answer: Daily life topics such as work and hobbies.

meaningEn: instead of
examples: Walk rather than drive when possible.

Practice speaking rather than only reading.

conversation.question: Should we rewrite everything?
conversation.answer: Let us refactor rather than rewrite from scratch.

meaningEn: early enough for a purpose
examples: We arrived in time for the opening.
Finish revisions in time for review.
I learned the rule just in time.

conversation.question: Will the report be ready?
conversation.answer: Yes, I will finish it in time for the meeting.

meaningEn: able to be used or obtained
examples: Is the manager available now?
More seats are available online.
Help is available whenever you need it.

conversation.question: Can we meet this afternoon?
conversation.answer: I am available after three.

meaningEn: easy to use or suitable for your needs
examples: Online classes are convenient for workers.
Is this time convenient for you?
The app has a convenient search feature.

conversation.question: Can we move the lesson to Sunday?
conversation.answer: Sunday morning would be more convenient for me.

meaningEn: feeling sure about your abilities
examples: She feels confident in meetings now.
Practice makes learners more confident.
I am confident we can fix this bug.

conversation.question: Are you ready to present?
conversation.answer: Yes, I feel confident after rehearsing.

meaningEn: able to be trusted to work well
examples: We need a reliable internet connection.
He is a reliable teammate.
This method is simple and reliable.

conversation.question: Can we use this old library?
conversation.answer: Yes, it is reliable and well documented.

meaningEn: easy to see or understand
examples: The answer was obvious after the hint.

conversation.question: Did you spot the mistake quickly?
conversation.answer: Yes, it was obvious once I read it aloud.
